title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Colonel Melrose interrogates the footman, Jenkins, regarding his whereabouts. Jenkins states he brought his master soft leather slippers and took away a pair of brogues, leaving his master after 6:15 and returning to the servants' quarters by 6:30. Curtis confirms Jenkins was in the servants' room from approximately 6:20 to 7:00, effectively clearing him of suspicion.

An anxious maid announces that Laura DeWitt wishes to see the Colonel. Laura appears in a navy blue brocade tea gown, looking unsteady. After being led into a sitting room by Colonel Melrose, she suddenly confesses to shooting Sir James de Witt downstairs. When Mr. Quincannon asks about the pistol, she becomes agitated, claiming she either dropped it or threw it out a window, though she admits she cannot remember clearly. As she leaves, the maid expresses concern that Jenkins might be suspected.

Following Laura's departure, a man named Paul Danglars arrives. He possesses an elegant, theatrical bearing. Danglars confesses to the murder, claiming he stabbed Sir James in the heart with a gem-encrusted paper knife that he snatched from a book belonging to Lady DeWitt. He explains that he entered and exited through a window around 6:30, after chatting with the gamekeeper at 6:15. Colonel Melrose notes the peculiarity of the case, informing the stunned Danglars that Mrs. DeWitt has also come forward with a confession.
